Title: An (E,E)-α-farnesene synthase gene of soybean has a role in defence against nematodes and is involved in synthesizing insect-induced volatiles

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes (E,E)-α-farnesene biosynthesis and enhances resistance to soybean cyst nematode when overexpressed; contributes to herbivory-induced volatile emission.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS-b subfamily); contains DDxxD motif, NSD/DTE motif, and H-α1 loop
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/converts farnesyl diphosphate into the sesquiterpene hydrocarbon (E,E)-α-farnesene in a K+ and Mg2+-dependent reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defence-related volatile; implicated in defence against soybean cyst nematode and in indirect defence by attracting natural enemies of herbivores.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves (emitted volatile blend after Tetranychus urticae infestation); roots implicated in defence against SCN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression in Escherichia coli and enzyme assay; GC-MS product identification; qRT-PCR expression analysis; transgenic hairy root overexpression; SCN bioassay; plant volatile profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean cyst nematode infection; Tetranychus urticae infestation; exogenous methyl jasmonate treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots of SCN-resistant line TN02-226 after SCN infection; not induced in susceptible TN02-275 roots; induced in leaves by spider mite feeding and methyl jasmonate; previously reported high in stem and mature flowers and strongly induced by wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots of TN02-226 by SCN infection; induced in Williams 82 leaves by T. urticae and methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering soybean with improved resistance to SCN and potentially enhanced indirect insect defence through volatile emission; suitable for transgenic overexpression and possibly gene stacking with other defence genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix GeneChip microarray; probe set Gma.625.1.S1_at
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAFS encodes a TPS-b family sesquiterpene synthase that converts FPP to (E,E)-α-farnesene in the presence of K+ and Mg2+. 2. GmAFS expression is specifically induced by SCN infection in an SCN-resistant soybean line but not in a susceptible line. 3. Overexpression of GmAFS in Williams 82 transgenic hairy roots reduced SCN susceptibility by about 40%. 4. GmAFS is induced in leaves by spider mite herbivory and methyl jasmonate, and (E,E)-α-farnesene is a major induced volatile emitted from infested plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous and structurally similar to apple MdAFS and poplar PtTPS2; unlike most TPS-b members, functions as a sesquiterpene synthase and requires K+ for activity; also referred to as GmTPS21.
